response.original_title: Generational: The Story Of Asian Roots Collective
response.overview: In 1995, two friends, Clement Chu and David Kuo, had a simple vision: to give Chinese-Canadian youth a place to belong through basketball. They started the Canadian Chinese Youth Athletic Association (CCYAA), which quickly grew into something much bigger than just basketball. Today, rebranded as the Asian Roots Collective (ARC), their work has expanded far beyond the court, becoming one of Toronto's most influential non-profits.
response.title: Generational: The Story Of Asian Roots Collective
